reviews.author: Harry Yoo
reviews.summary: Reviewer Harry Yoo noted that SLUB had a similar issue where it used prandom if spinning was not allowed when shuffling slab freelist, and suggested applying a similar fix to the current patch
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks!

FYI, SLUB had a similar issue that was recently fixed:
commit a1e244a9f1778 ("mm/slab: use prandom if !allow_spin").

It uses prandom if spinning is not allowed when shuffling slab freelist.

-- 
Cheers,
Harry / Hyeonggon

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the drain mechanism, considering alternatives like round-robin to simplify the code and avoid exposing corner cases.
reviews.raw_body:
The drain does not really need a random number. Fixing an index like 0 makes it
much simpler but it might expose some corner cases. Round robin might be enough
for this though. I will see what would be the easiest way forward.

2026-05-21.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a performance regression caused by the introduction of per-node obj_cgroups, which led to thrashing in the per-CPU cache. The patches shrink the existing single-slot obj_stock_pcp fields and convert cached_objcg and nr_bytes into slot arrays to reduce cache footprint. A more general approach is taken to cache multiple objcgs per CPU using a multi-slot pattern, allowing sibling per-node objcgs to coexist without forcing a drain. This series aims to revert the temporary fix introduced earlier and improve performance on workloads with threads running on different NUMA nodes.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Reviewer Joshua Hahn noted that his previous work to push the memcg stock to page_counter eliminates the random eviction and get_random_u32_below issues, but suggested abstracting or pushing down the objcg stock to a finer granularity than per-node to avoid random evictions. He also offered a simple fix for get_random_32_below using prandom_u32_state.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Harry and Shakeel!

I just wanted to note that my work to push the memcg stock to page_counter [1]
actually gets rid of the random eviction and also the get_random_u32_below.
So the problem will no longer exist, at least for memcg/page_counter stock.

I was also thinking whether there was any way we can abstract or push down the
objcg stock to a different per_X slot (with finer granularity than per-node)
so we don't do random evictions, but it doesn't seem like there's a natural
point to do this from a quick glance.

The fix for get_random_32_below to use prandom_u32_state seems quite simple
though, so if either of you would like to send that in as a small hotfix
it shouldn't be a big deal to rebase on top of that instead too.

(Sorry for joining the conversation late too. I'll take a closer look at this
entire series tomorrow as well).

Thank you both. Have a great day!
Joshua

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260410210742.550489-1-joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com/

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a performance regression caused by the introduction of per-node obj_cgroups, which led to thrashing in the per-CPU cache. The patches shrink the existing single-slot obj_stock_pcp fields and convert cached_objcg and nr_bytes into slot arrays to reduce cache footprint. A more general approach is taken to cache multiple objcgs per CPU using a multi-slot pattern, allowing sibling per-node objcgs to coexist without forcing a drain. This series aims to revert the temporary fix introduced earlier and improve performance on workloads with threads running on different NUMA nodes.
